questions.q: Name the part of a flower which bears stamens.
What is the process called, when pollen grains germinate to form a pollen tube and a generative cell?
The part of the flower that bears ovules is called the _______________.
Distinguish between monocots and dicots, on the basis of the number of cotyledons present in the seed.
questions.answer: Monocots have one cotyledon while dicots have two cotyledons.

questions.q: What is the function of the stigma in a flower?
questions.answer: The stigma is the sticky surface at the top of the style that catches the pollen grains.

questions.q: What is the characteristic feature of a flower that is bilaterally symmetrical?
questions.answer: The characteristic feature of a bilaterally symmetrical flower is that it has two equal halves when divided in the middle.

questions.q: What is the name of the part of the flower that helps in reproduction?
questions.answer: Reproductive part of the flower is the _______________.

questions.q: Mention the different types of pollination.
questions.answer: There are two main types of pollination, namely self-pollination and cross-pollination.

questions.q: Describe the structure of a typical monocot stem.
questions.answer: A typical monocot stem has a ring of vascular bundles, with xylem present towards the outer side and phloem towards the inner side.

questions.q: What is the function of the calyx in a flower?
questions.answer: The calyx is the outermost whorl of the flower and helps in protection.
